description: Read items from stdin and execute a command for each one.

platform.value_on_windows: No native xargs; Git Bash xargs has path-mangling issues with Windows paths
platform.value_on_unix: Sane line-delimited default instead of whitespace splitting

options.description: Structured JSON metadata for AI agents
Streaming NDJSON per job to stderr
POSIX whitespace splitting with quote handling
Print each command to stderr before running
Disable shell fallback for builtins (cmd /c, sh -c)
Coloured output: auto (default when omitted), always, or never

exit_codes.description: One or more child processes failed (or could not be spawned). Per-job spawn failures surface in fault_message rather than as a separate exit code — wargs intentionally collapses spawn failures into 123 + per-job diagnostic.
Internal/IO failure: stdin read failed, unexpected exception escaped to safety net

io.stdin: Items to process, one per line (default). Null-delimited with -0. Whitespace with --compat.
io.stdout: Child process stdout (buffered per job by default)
io.stderr: Failure summary. JSON with --json. NDJSON per job with --ndjson.

json_output_fields.name: tool
json_output_fields.description: Tool name ("wargs"). Emitted in both --json summary envelope and --ndjson per-job lines.

json_output_fields.name: version
json_output_fields.description: Tool version. Emitted in both --json summary envelope and --ndjson per-job lines.

json_output_fields.name: exit_code
json_output_fields.description: (--json summary) Tool exit code: 0 = success; 123 = child_failed (any child non-zero exit OR spawn failure — spawn failures surface via per-job fault_message rather than a separate exit code); 124 = fail_fast_abort; 125 = usage_error; 126 = unexpected_error / input_read_failed; 130 = cancelled. (--ndjson per-job) Restricted to 0 (job succeeded) or 123 (job's child failed or could not be spawned).

json_output_fields.name: exit_reason
json_output_fields.description: (--json summary) One of: success, child_failed, fail_fast_abort, no_input, input_read_failed, usage_error, dry_run, cancelled, unexpected_error. (--ndjson per-job) Restricted to: success or child_failed.

json_output_fields.name: total_jobs
json_output_fields.description: (--json summary) Total number of jobs. Reflects --dry-run plan count when emitted under exit_reason=dry_run.

json_output_fields.name: skipped
json_output_fields.description: (--json summary) Jobs skipped (fail-fast or confirm declined)

json_output_fields.name: wall_seconds
json_output_fields.description: (--json summary) Wall-clock duration of the wargs run in seconds — NOT the sum of per-job durations (under -P parallel jobs overlap, so wall_seconds is closer to max(per-job durations) plus dispatch overhead). (--ndjson per-job) Per-job wall-clock duration in seconds.

json_output_fields.name: faults
json_output_fields.description: (--json summary) Per-job fault diagnostics; present only when at least one job carries a FaultMessage. Each entry is {job: int, message: string}.

json_output_fields.name: job
json_output_fields.description: (--ndjson per-job) 1-based job index of this line

json_output_fields.name: child_exit_code
json_output_fields.description: (--ndjson per-job) Child process exit code. -1 only on a TRUE spawn failure (--no-shell-fallback, or the fallback shell itself failing to start) — under the default shell fallback a not-found command is retried via sh -c / cmd /c, so the shell's exit code (127 / 9009) appears here instead, with no fault_message.

json_output_fields.name: input
json_output_fields.description: (--ndjson per-job) Source item(s) — string when --batch=1, array when batched

json_output_fields.name: fault_message
json_output_fields.description: (--ndjson per-job) Spawn/task fault diagnostic; omitted on normal paths
